title: PUT /books/{id} performs no validation — required fields can be blanked
evidence: handlers.go:175 HandleUpdateBook decodes UpdateBookRequest and calls db.UpdateBook without calling validateCreate; database.go:149-160 applies title/author verbatim, so PUT {"title":""} sets an empty required field
suggestion: Validate title/author non-empty on update when the field is provided, mirroring validateCreate

title: ?author= filter uses substring LIKE rather than exact match
evidence: database.go:88-89 builds "WHERE author LIKE ?" with args "%"+authorFilter+"%", so ?author=Herbert also matches 'Frank Herbertson'
suggestion: Use author = ? for exact-match semantics, or document the substring behavior

title: doRequest test helper double-invokes handlers for /health
evidence: handler_test.go:36-40 calls s.HandleBooks(w,r) then, if path=="/health", also calls s.HandleHealth(w,r) on the same ResponseWriter — HandleBooks first writes a 404 for the unknown path
suggestion: Route /health exclusively to HandleHealth in the helper instead of after HandleBooks

title: Health timestamp is a Unix-epoch string, not RFC3339
evidence: handlers.go:29 sets Timestamp: fmt.Sprintf("%d", time.Now().Unix()); model.go:47 types it as string
suggestion: Emit an RFC3339 timestamp (time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339)) for readability/consistency

title: Partial update supported via pointer DTO fields
evidence: model.go:25-30 UpdateBookRequest uses *string/*int so absent fields are preserved; verified by handler_test.go:326 TestUpdateBookPartial
suggestion: None — beyond-spec quality signal; not a deduction
